title: Dense Middle-Eastern Lamb Mansaf
description: A hearty and comforting lamb dish, characterized by its rich flavor and velvety texture, originating from the Middle East.

ingredients.name: Pomegranate molasses
ingredients.notes: Middle Eastern ingredient, can be substituted with a mixture of equal parts molasses and pomegranate juice

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: flaky sea salt, such as Maldon

instructions: Score the lamb shoulder in a diamond pattern, cutting about 1/4 inch deep, being careful not to cut all the way through.
Place the lamb shoulder on a wire rack set over a large baking sheet or roasting pan, and let it air-dry in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours, or overnight.
Remove the lamb from the refrigerator and rub it all over with the minced garlic and grated ginger.
Season the lamb with cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, making sure to get some of the spice mixture into the cuts you made earlier.
Transfer the lamb to a bain-marie setup by placing the baking sheet or roasting pan over a larger pot or Dutch oven filled with water.
Cover the pot with a lid and bring the water to a simmer over medium heat.
Once the water is simmering, transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the lamb for about 2 1/2 hours, or until it's tender and easily shreds with a fork.
While the lamb is braising, prepare the hummus by stirring in the pomegranate molasses until well combined.
Warm the pita bread according to package instructions and slice it into wedges.
To serve, place a generous portion of the lamb on a platter, spoon some of the hummus over the top, and garnish with chopped fresh parsley and a sprinkle of sumac, if desired.
Serve with a side of fluffy white rice and pita wedges, and enjoy!
